requirements.title: Base ISO standards
requirements.text: A PDF file shall be compliant to the ISO standard(s): <A>. In case of (perceived) conflict, the requirements of the base ISO standards shall always take precedence over any requirements defined here.

requirements.title: Page scaling
requirements.text: No page dictionary in the PDF file shall contain the UserUnit key.

requirements.title: Visible page area
requirements.text: For all pages in the PDF file, the CropBox shall coincide with the MediaBox.
requirements.notes: This can be accomplished by explicitly defining the CropBox with the same values as the MediaBox, or by omitting the CropBox (in which case it falls back to the values defined in the MediaBox).

requirements.title: Same page size and orientation
requirements.text: The size of the TrimBox shall be the same for all pages in the PDF file. No pages in the PDF file shall be rotated through the use of the Rotate key.

requirements.title: Empty page
requirements.text: No page in the PDF file shall be completely empty. A page is considered empty if the Total Ink Coverage (D0027) is equal to zero.

requirements.title: One page
requirements.text: The number of pages in the PDF file shall be exactly one (1).

requirements.title: Overprinting white text
requirements.text:
An Overlapping (D0031) Text Element (D0005) that is set to fill, or stroke and fill, and uses a White Fill Colour (D0011), shall not use Fill Overprint (D0020). 
An Overlapping (D0031) Text Element (D0005) that is set to stroke, or stroke and fill, and uses a White Stroke Colour (D0012), shall not use Stroke Overprint (D0021).

requirements.title: Overprinting white paths
requirements.text:
An Overlapping (D0031) Path Element (D0004) that is set to fill, or stroke and fill, and uses a White Fill Colour (D0011), shall not use Fill Overprint (D0020). 
An Overlapping (D0031) Path Element (D0004) that is set to stroke, or stroke and fill, and uses a White Stroke Colour (D0012), shall not use Stroke Overprint (D0021).

requirements.title: Overprinting pure black text
requirements.text:
An Overlapping (D0031) Text Element (D0005) that is set to fill, or stroke and fill, and uses a Black Fill Colour (D0014) and has an Effective Font Size (D0019) smaller than <A> point shall use Fill Overprint (D0020).
An Overlapping (D0031) Text Element that is set to stroke, or stroke and fill, and uses a Black Stroke Colour (D0015) and has an Effective Font Size (D0019) smaller than <A> point shall use Stroke Overprint (D0021).

- If the colour space in use for either of the above rules is defined as DeviceCMYK, the value of the OPM key additionally shall be set to 1.
- If the colour space in use for the fill or stroke part of the above rules is defined as DeviceGray, that rule shall not apply at all.

requirements.title: Overprinting pure black thin lines
requirements.text:
An Overlapping (D0031) Path Element (D0004) that is filled, and uses Black Fill Colour (D0014), and has an Effective Line Width (D0017) less than <A>, shall use Fill Overprint (D0020).
An Overlapping (D0031) Path Element (D0004) that is stroked, and uses Black Stroke Colour (D0015), and has an Effective Line Width (D0017) less than <A>, shall use Stroke Overprint (D0021).

- If the colour space in use for either of the above rules is defined as DeviceCMYK, the value of the OPM key additionally shall be set to 1.
- If the colour space in use for the fill or stroke part of the above rules is defined as DeviceGray, that rule shall not apply at all.

requirements.title: Overprinting pure black small text in DeviceGray
requirements.text: An Overlapping (D0031) Text Element (D0005) with an Effective Font Size (D0019) less than <A> shall not have a stroke colour space of DeviceGray if it is stroked, and it shall not have a fill colour space of DeviceGray if it is filled.
requirements.notes: Even though this requirement does not mention overprint at all, the requirement is still correct. In the case of small text using DeviceGray, *both* overprint and knockout are in fact wrong. Overprint is wrong because it will not always be honoured for DeviceGray; knockout is always wrong for small black text (for registration reasons).

requirements.title: Overprinting pure black thin lines in DeviceGray
requirements.text: An Overlapping (D0031) Path Element (D0004) with an Effective Line Width (D0017) less than <A>, shall not have a stroke colour space of DeviceGray if it is stroked, and it shall not have a fill colour space of DeviceGray if it is filled.
requirements.notes: Even though this requirement does not mention overprint at all, the requirement is still correct. In the case of thin lines using DeviceGray, *both* overprint and knockout are in fact wrong. Overprint is wrong because it will not always be honoured for DeviceGray; knockout is always wrong for thin black lines (for registration reasons).

requirements.title: Overprinting device gray
requirements.text:
An Overlapping (D0031) Page Element (D0003) that is filled and has DeviceGray as fill colour space shall not use Fill Overprint (D0020).
An Overlapping (D0031) Page Element (D0003) that is stroked and has DeviceGray as stroke colour space shall not use Stroke Overprint (D0021).

requirements.title: Use of the Courier font
requirements.text: Any Text Element (D0005) shall not use a font with the name "Courier". This requirement does not restrict the use of fonts with slightly different names such as for example "Courier New".
requirements.notes: This requirement exists because the font "Courier" was the default replacement font of PostScript to PDF tools, and as such can indicate an unwanted font substitution took place.

requirements.title: Rich black text
requirements.text: A Visible (D0030) Text Element (D0005) that is using a fill colour space that is DeviceCMYK or DeviceN (where the DeviceN colour space has at least two process colour components, one of which being Black), shall not have a Black colour component larger than or equal to <A>, if the sum of all process colour components for that Text Element (D0005) is larger than <B>.

requirements.title: Registration problems with small white text
requirements.text: An Overlapping (D0031) Text Element (D0005) shall not have an Effective Font Size (D0019) smaller than <A> if it uses a White Fill Colour (D0011).

requirements.title: Registration problems with small multi-channel text
requirements.text: A Text Element (D0005) shall not have an Effective Font Size (D0019) smaller than <A> if more than one of its colour components have a value greater than 0.

requirements.title: Registration problems with thin white lines
requirements.text: An Overlapping (D0031) Path Element (D0004) shall not have an Effective Line Width (D0017) smaller than <A> if the colour used to calculate the Effective Line Width (D0017) is a White Colour (D0010).

requirements.title: Registration problems with thin multi-channel lines
requirements.text: A Path Element (D0004) shall not have an Effective Line Width (D0017) smaller than <A> if more than 1 of the colour components of the colour space used to calculate the Effective Line Width (D0017) have a value greater than 0.

requirements.title: Use of spot colours
requirements.text: A PDF file shall have no more than <A> different names in the Active Spot Colour Names (D0024).

requirements.title: Spot colours with different suffixes
requirements.text:
The Active Spot Colour Names (D0024) collection in a PDF shall not contain names that are different but equivalent. Equivalence is considered for those spot colours where the name of the spot colour ends in a SPACE character (20h) followed by a single one of these character combinations: C, CP, E, K, M, N, U, UP, Z, CV, CVU, CVC, HC, PC, TC, TCX, TP, TPX, XGC, EC.

For all spot colour names that comply with the above requirement, a case-insensitive string comparison is done on the string consisting of all characters before the last SPACE character identified by the above requirement. String equality is then identical to spot colour name equivalence.

requirements.title: Case-sensitive spot colour names
requirements.text: The Active Spot Colour Names (D0024) collection in a PDF shall not contain names that are only different in their capitalization or that are canonically equivalent in Unicode. Canonically equivalent means that the unicode string would be identical after decomposition and/or precomposition.

requirements.title: Different spot colours that are visually identical
requirements.text: The Active Spot Colours (D0023) in a PDF file shall not contain spot colours that have the same tintTransform and alternateSpace but different names. In evaluating equivalence, PDF objects shall be compared rather than the computational result of the use of those objects, but the difference between direct or indirect objects shall be ignored.

requirements.title: Registration colour
requirements.text: No Page Element (D0003) shall use a Spot Colour Name (D0022) equal to "All".

requirements.title: Average ink coverage of all separations
requirements.text: Ink coverage of all separations (D00027) shall not exceed the average value <A>, within any square area with side <B>.

requirements.title: Average ink coverage of CMYK separations
requirements.text: Ink coverage of CMYK separations (D00028) shall not exceed the average value <A>, within any square area with side <B>.

requirements.title: Early binding mode
requirements.text:
No Page Element (D0003) shall use the following colour spaces as intended or alternate colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- CalRGB
- CalGray
- ICCBased (ICC based grayscale, RGB, Lab and CMYK)

No Page Element (D0003) shall use the following colour space as an intended colour space:
- Lab

requirements.title: Intermediate binding mode
requirements.text:
Page Elements (D0003) that are Image Elements (D0006) but not Image Mask Elements (D0009), shall not use the following colour spaces as intended colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- ICCBasedGray
- CalGray
- ICCBasedCMYK

Page Elements (D0003) that are not Image Elements (D0006), or that are Image Mask Elements (D0009), shall not use the following colour space as an intended colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- CalRGB
- CalGray
- ICCBased (ICC based grayscale, RGB, Lab and CMYK)
- Lab

Page Elements (D0003) shall not use the following colour space as an alternate colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- ICCBasedGray
- CalGray
- ICCBasedCMYK

requirements.title: Late binding mode
requirements.text:
Page Elements (D0003) that are Image Elements (D0006) but not Image Mask Elements (D0009), shall not use the following colour spaces as intended colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- ICCBasedGray
- CalGray

Page Elements (D0003) that are not Image Elements (D0006), or that are Image Mask Elements (D0009), shall not use the following colour space as an intended colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- CalRGB
- CalGray
- ICCbasedCMYK
- ICCbasedGray

Page Elements (D0003) shall not use the following colour space as an alternate colour space:
- DeviceRGB
- ICCBasedGray
- CalGray

requirements.title: Transparency blend colour space
requirements.text: In a transparency group attributes dictionary, if the CS key is present, it shall have the value DeviceCMYK. As an exception to this rule, if the transparency group attributes dictionary is the value of the G key in a Luminosity sub-type soft mask dictionary, the value of the CS key shall either be DeviceCMYK or DeviceGray.

requirements.title: Image resolution for grayscale and colour images
requirements.text: The Effective Image Resolution (D0018) of a Continuous-Tone Image Element (D0008) not covered by the requirement on Rasterized pages (R0033), shall be greater than <A> for images with a width or height greater than 16 pixels.

requirements.title: Image resolution for 1-bit images
requirements.text: The Effective Image Resolution (D0018) of a 1-Bit Image Element (D0007), shall be greater than <A> for images with a width or height greater than 16 pixels.

requirements.title: Rasterized pages
requirements.text: If a page in a PDF file contains only one Page Element (D0003), which is a Continuous-Tone Image Element (D0008) that covers the TrimBox, its Effective Image Resolution (D0018) shall be greater than <A>.

requirements.title: Optional content
requirements.text: If the document catalog dictionary contains an optional content properties dictionary as the value of the OCProperties key, the optional content properties dictionary shall not contain a Configs key.

requirements.title: Output intent colour space
requirements.text: The colour space of the ICC Profile that is the destination profile in PDF/X output intent object(s), shall be CMYK.

requirements.title: Hidden optional content
requirements.text: If the document contains an optional content properties dictionary as the value of the D entry in the OCProperties entry of the document catalog dictionary, it shall be configured so that the initial state of all optional content groups it references is ON.
requirements.notes: This requirement means that all optional content groups in the document must be visible (and thus print). Make note that this requirement complements the requirements already present in the Base ISO Standards (R0001) for the variant where this requirement is used.

requirements.title: Scaling factor and viewing distance
requirements.text: If the variant table contains a value of "Warning" or "Error" for this requirement, the Scaling Factor (D0025) and Viewing Distance (D0026) will be taken into account in the defintions for Effective Line Width (D0017), Effective Image Resolution (D0018), and Effective Font Size (D0019).

requirements.title: Use of processing steps
requirements.text:
A PDF that corresponds with a certain Product Type (D0029) shall contain all processing steps that are marked as "required" in the variants table. Such a PDF shall not contain a processing step that is marked as "not allowed" in that variant table.

Any processing step marked as "recommended" in the variant table should be present in the document but may be reported.

requirements.title: Required creasing
requirements.text:
The PDF file shall contain at least one of the Processing Steps defined below:
- GTS_ProcStepsGroup = "Structural", GTS_ProcStepsType = "Creasing"
- GTS_ProcStepsGroup = "Structural", GTS_ProcStepsType = "ReverseCreasing"
